gargantua.warningContent: The Gargantua theme includes a real-time ray tracing simulation based on physical shaders, which will significantly increase power consumption and may cause the device to heat up. Please enable with caution.
gargantua.aboutContent:
Gargantua is a flagship experimental visual theme inspired by the scientific simulation of a black hole in the movie Interstellar. It is not just a skin, but a complete UI reinvention designed to transform your task management experience into an immersive journey through spacetime.

Technical Core:
- **Real-time Rendering**: The background is a black hole rendered in real-time via GLSL shaders based on general relativity formulas. Every frame of light is precisely calculated to simulate gravitational lensing and the Doppler effect of the accretion disk.
- **Physical Accuracy**: Simulation parameters (such as camera revolution speed, light step precision) are adjustable, allowing you to control this miniature universe yourself.
- **Terminology Reconstruction**: From "Origin" to "Terminal", from "Dawn-Orbit" to "Warp Drive", all terms have been replaced to build a complete "spacetime navigation" worldview.

smartPlan.title: TARS
soups: Do not go gentle into that good night 🌌
Love transcends time and space ✨
One hour here, seven years on Earth ⏳
Honesty 90%, 10% for diplomacy 🤖
Murphy's Law: Anything wrong, will go wrong ⚛️
We used to look up at the stars 🔭
Now we just look down at the dust 🌍
Go beyond our own horizon 🛸
Gravity crosses dimensions ☄️
Time is a physical dimension 🌀

world_tb_day.name: World TB Day
world_tb_day.desc: World Tuberculosis Day (World TB Day) is observed annually on March 24 to raise public awareness about the devastating health, social, and economic consequences of tuberculosis (TB) and to step up efforts to end the global TB epidemic.
world_tb_day.reason: The theme colors are pure white and warning red. White represents the sterile clinical environment and healthy lungs, while red symbolizes vitality, vigilance, and the global determination to fight TB. The entire UI is reshaped into a precise clinical observation interface.

facts.1: Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, which most often affects the lungs.
facts.2: TB is spread through the air when people with TB cough, sneeze, or spit.
facts.3: About a quarter of the global population is infected with TB, but most will not develop the disease.
facts.4: TB is one of the top 10 causes of death worldwide and the leading killer of people with HIV.

world_water_day.name: World Water Day
world_water_day.desc: World Water Day is an annual United Nations observance day held on 22 March that highlights the importance of fresh water.
world_water_day.reason: The theme colors are inspired by the various states of water: deep ocean blue, clear lake green, and pure glacier white. The gradient simulates light changes from deep sea to shallows, creating a fresh, tranquil visual experience.

facts.1: Less than 1% of the world's freshwater is accessible for direct human use.
facts.2: A leaking tap can waste up to 75 liters of water per day.
facts.3: Producing a 500ml bottle of water takes more than 3 times the amount of water to make.
facts.4: About 2.2 billion people live without access to safe water.
facts.5: Agriculture accounts for 70% of global freshwater withdrawals.

lunar_new_year.name: Lunar New Year
lunar_new_year.tagline: Out with the old, in with the new
lunar_new_year.desc: Lunar New Year, also known as Spring Festival, is the most important traditional festival for Chinese people. It marks the beginning of the lunar calendar year.
lunar_new_year.reason: Centered around Chinese Red, representing joy, luck, and warding off evil; Golden Yellow as secondary, symbolizing wealth and prosperity.

facts.1: Chunyun, the Spring Festival travel season, is the world's largest annual human migration.
facts.2: About 90% of the world's fireworks are consumed during Chinese New Year.
facts.3: The 'Fu' character is posted upside down to mean 'Good luck has arrived'.
facts.4: New Year's Eve dinner dishes have symbolic meanings, like fish for abundance.

mid_autumn.name: Mid-Autumn Festival
mid_autumn.tagline: Family reunion under the full moon
mid_autumn.desc: The Mid-Autumn Festival is a traditional festival celebrated in Chinese culture. It is named for the fact that the moon is at its roundest and brightest at this time.
mid_autumn.reason: Deep night sky background with moon-gold as the primary color, recreating the blue sky and moonlight of the Mid-Autumn night.

facts.1: It is one of the four most important traditional Chinese festivals.
facts.2: Similar moon-watching festivals are celebrated in Vietnam, Japan, and Korea.
facts.3: Mooncakes are the traditional food of this festival.
facts.4: The festival is associated with the legend of Chang'e, the Moon Goddess.

Don't compare yourself to yesterday, just be better than 5 mins ago ⏰

Every task completed is a tiny reward for your brain 🧠✨
Action is the best cure for procrastination. Start for 3 mins ⚡
Done is better than perfect—perfection is always in progress 🎯

Every tree you planted yesterday is growing today 🌱
Focus is not a gift, it's a series of choices 🔥
Your task list is a promise to your future self 📋

dragon_boat.name: Dragon Boat Festival
dragon_boat.desc:
The Dragon Boat Festival is one of the four major traditional Chinese festivals, originating from the worship of natural celestial phenomena and evolving from the dragon sacrifice in ancient times. In the midsummer, the seven mansions of the Azure Dragon fly to the center of the southern sky, in the most "central and upright" position of the year, as described in the fifth yao of the Qian hexagram in the I Ching: "Flying dragon in the sky."

The origin of the Dragon Boat Festival covers ancient astrological culture, humanistic philosophy, and other aspects, containing profound cultural connotations. In its inheritance and development, it has mixed a variety of folk customs, with rich festival content. Dragon boat racing and eating zongzi are the two major customs of the Dragon Boat Festival, which have been passed down in China since ancient times and continue to this day.
dragon_boat.reason: The theme color is extracted from the most representative elements of the Dragon Boat Festival: the deep green of zongzi leaves, the light green of mugwort, and the warm atmosphere of dragon boat racing. The overall color tone is fresh and natural, bringing a touch of coolness and vitality in summer.

facts.1: The Dragon Boat Festival, along with the Spring Festival, Qingming Festival, and Mid-Autumn Festival, is known as one of the four major traditional Chinese festivals.
facts.2: In September 2009, UNESCO officially approved its inclusion in the Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity, making the Dragon Boat Festival the first Chinese festival to be selected as a world intangible cultural heritage.
facts.3: The Dragon Boat Festival has more than 20 alternative names, such as Duanyang Festival, Chongwu Festival, Dragon Boat Festival, Dragon Day Festival, Zhengyang Festival, Yulan Festival, and Tianzhong Festival.
facts.4: In addition to eating zongzi and racing dragon boats, there are also customs such as hanging mugwort, wearing perfume sachets, drinking realgar wine, and tying five-color silk threads, which are mostly related to warding off evil spirits and epidemics.
facts.5: Although the most famous legend is to commemorate Qu Yuan, in different regions, the Dragon Boat Festival is also used to commemorate Wu Zixu, Cao E, or Jie Zitui.

world_tb_day.name: World Tuberculosis Day
world_tb_day.desc:
World Tuberculosis Day is observed on March 24 each year to raise public awareness about the devastating health, social and economic consequences of tuberculosis (TB) and to step up efforts to end the global TB epidemic.

On this day in 1882, Dr. Robert Koch announced that he had discovered the bacterium that causes TB, which opened the way towards diagnosing and curing this disease. Although preventable and curable, TB remains one of the world’s deadliest infectious killers.
world_tb_day.reason: The theme color uses pure white and warning red. White represents the sterile clinical environment and healthy lungs, while red symbolizes the vitality of life, vigilance, and the global determination to fight TB. The entire UI is reshaped into a sophisticated clinical observation interface.

facts.1: Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by a bacterium called Mycobacterium tuberculosis. The bacteria usually attack the lungs, but TB bacteria can attack any part of the body such as the kidney, spine, and brain.
facts.2: TB is spread from person to person through the air. When people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit, they propel the TB germs into the air.
facts.3: About a quarter of the world's population is infected with TB bacteria. Only a small proportion of those infected will become sick with TB. People with weakened immune systems have a much greater risk of falling ill.

world_water_day.name: World Water Day
world_water_day.desc:
World Water Day was established by the United Nations in 1993 to promote the integrated planning and management of water resources, strengthen water resource protection, and address the increasingly severe problem of freshwater scarcity.

Every year on March 22, various events are held around the world to raise public awareness of water conservation and strengthen the protection of water resources. Every drop of water is crucial, save water, starting with you and me.
world_water_day.reason: The theme color is inspired by the various forms of water: the deep blue of the ocean, the clear green of the lake, and the pure white of the glacier. The gradient color simulates the change of light and shadow from the deep sea to the shallows, creating a refreshing and tranquil visual experience.

facts.1: Less than 1% of the Earth's freshwater is directly available for human use.
facts.2: A leaking faucet can waste up to 75 liters of water per day.
facts.3: Producing a 500ml bottle of bottled water requires more than 3 times its own volume of water.
facts.4: About 2.2 billion people worldwide lack access to safe drinking water services.
facts.5: Agriculture is the largest water user in the world, accounting for about 70% of global freshwater withdrawals.

dragon_boat.name: Dragon Boat Festival
dragon_boat.dateLabel: 5th day of the 5th lunar month (late May to early June)
dragon_boat.tagline: Zongzi aroma and racing dragon boats
dragon_boat.desc: The Dragon Boat Festival (Duanwu) falls on the 5th day of the 5th lunar month. It is widely believed to commemorate the poet Qu Yuan. Traditions include dragon boat racing and eating zongzi (sticky rice dumplings). Today, dragon boat races are held in many countries around the world.
dragon_boat.reason: Leafy green (#2A9C72) as the primary, deep water green (#1A5C3A) for text, and lighter green accents (#5DC49A) over a misty pond backdrop (#EEF8F4) — conveying freshness and calm.

facts.1: Inscribed on UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage list (2009)
facts.2: Dragon boat races attract tens of millions worldwide each year
facts.3: Zongzi recipes vary greatly across regions
facts.4: Racing drums synchronize dozens of paddlers in one rhythm
facts.5: Hanging mugwort and wearing five-color cords symbolize blessings

world_tb_day.name: World Tuberculosis Day
world_tb_day.desc:
World Tuberculosis Day is observed on March 24 each year to raise public awareness about the devastating health, social and economic consequences of tuberculosis (TB) and to step up efforts to end the global TB epidemic.

On this day in 1882, Dr. Robert Koch announced that he had discovered the bacterium that causes TB, which opened the way towards diagnosing and curing this disease. Although preventable and curable, TB remains one of the world’s deadliest infectious killers.
world_tb_day.reason: The theme uses pure white and warning red. White represents a sterile clinical environment and healthy lungs; red symbolizes vitality, vigilance, and global determination to fight TB. The UI is reshaped into a precise clinical observation interface.

facts.1: Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis. It most often affects the lungs but can affect other organs.
facts.2: TB spreads through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze, or spit, releasing bacteria into the air.
facts.3: About a quarter of the world’s population is infected with TB bacteria, but most do not become ill; risk increases when immunity is weakened.

world_water_day.name: World Water Day
world_water_day.tagline: Cherish every drop, cherish every minute
world_water_day.desc: Established by the UN in 1993 and observed on March 22. It raises awareness of freshwater and promotes sustainable water management. Only a tiny fraction of Earth's water is accessible freshwater.
world_water_day.reason: Deep ocean blue (#0E86B4) with clear aqua accents (#38C4C8) and a misty white-blue background (#EFF7FB) — clean, flowing, and pure.

facts.1: Over 2.2 billion people lack safely managed drinking water (UN 2023)
facts.2: A dripping faucet can waste ~19 liters per day
facts.3: 1 kg of beef may consume ~15,000 liters of water
facts.4: Most freshwater is locked in glaciers and groundwater
facts.5: SDG 6 calls for clean water and sanitation for all

lunar_new_year.name: Chinese New Year
lunar_new_year.dateLabel: 1st day of the 1st lunar month (late Jan to Feb)
lunar_new_year.desc: The most important traditional holiday for Chinese communities. Customs include couplets, fireworks, family reunion dinners, red envelopes, and visiting relatives. Celebrations last until the Lantern Festival.
lunar_new_year.reason: Vibrant red (#CC2020) for joy and blessings, with golden accents (#EE8820) symbolizing prosperity.

facts.1: The Spring Festival triggers the world's largest annual migration

mid_autumn.name: Mid-Autumn Festival
mid_autumn.dateLabel: 15th day of the 8th lunar month (Sep to Oct)
mid_autumn.tagline: Under the same bright full moon
mid_autumn.desc: Celebrated on the 15th day of the 8th lunar month. It symbolizes reunion, harvest, and gratitude. Traditions include moon-gazing and eating mooncakes in many flavors.
mid_autumn.reason: Deep night sky (#1A1230) paired with moon gold (#E8C060), recreating a serene, warm mid-autumn night.

facts.1: One of the four major traditional Chinese festivals
facts.2: Similar celebrations exist in other East Asian countries
facts.3: The legend of Chang'e is a cultural symbol

NLP Task Entry||Parse "Meeting tomorrow afternoon" into time/block/title||planned
Dependency Analysis (AI)||AI-inferred prerequisites and suggested optimal sequence||researching
LLM Integration||GPT/DeepSeek/Gemini/Ollama: analysis, breakdown, report generation||researching

ui.title: 🎨 UI & Experience
ui.items:
Theme Store||15+ presets, auto seasonal switch, support import/export JSON||implementing
Animation Upgrade||Confetti on completion, shared element transitions, scrolling numbers||implementing
Weather Background Effects||QWeather API + Rain/Snow/Dust/Fog particle effects||implementing
Ambient (Screen-off) FX||Candlelight particles + Meteors + Breathing light||implementing
Theme follows Weather||Sunny→Warm, Rainy→Deep Blue, Night→Dark; linked with weather service||planned
Personalized Today Header||Custom header content: Weather/Motivation/Daily Goal||planned
Task Detail Bottom Sheet||Tap task title for details: notes/focus time/history||planned
Gesture Enhancement||Swipe up to add, left to complete/defer, shake for Pomodoro||planned
Matrix Enhancement||Drag cards between quadrants; include in stats analysis||planned
Ambient Mode Upgrade||B&W minimalist and analog clock styles; auto-dimming at night||planned
Quick Light/Dark Toggle||One-tap switch in top bar; no need to enter settings||planned

notif.title: 🔔 Notifications
notif.items:
Persistent Notification Control||Real-time countdown + task name; keep pause state in sync||implementing
Daily Review Push||9 PM push: "Completed X today, Y pending for tomorrow"||planned
Smart Reminder Suggestion||Recommend best reminder times based on completion history||planned
Low Completion Warning||6 PM check: If rate < 30%, push "N tasks remaining"||planned
Due Date Countdown||Step-wise reminders at 24h and 2h before deadline||planned

platform.title: 📱 Platform & System
platform.items:
Desktop Widgets||Jetpack Glance: Pomodoro / Today's Tasks / Focus Stats widgets||planned
App Shortcuts||Long press icon: Start Focus/Add Task/Today shortcuts||planned
Local Backup||Export all tasks/settings to .lsz file in Downloads; full restore||planned
JSON/CSV Export||Export selected date range; import into Excel for analysis||planned
WebDAV Sync||Config Nutstore/Nextcloud; upload JSON snapshot on save||planned
QR Code Import/Export||Encode today's tasks into QR code for lightweight migration||planned
Biometric Lock||FaceID/Fingerprint/PIN verification on startup or return from background||planned
Accessibility Support||TalkBack semantics, font scaling, high contrast mode||planned
Achievement System||Unlock themes by reaching 7-day 100% completion or focus milestones||researching
iOS Support||iOS adaptation, WidgetKit, iCloud sync||later
Multi-language||English/Japanese UI||later

changelogData.beta_0_084:
Fixed i18n path resolution logic: Removed incorrect themes. prefix to match JSON structure
Refactored Festivals translation structure: Moved translations to screens.festivals top-level path to fix UI raw key issues
Fixed Roadmap translation paths: Ensured roadmapData hierarchy matches code calls
Completed missing translations: Including weather background settings, roadmap section titles, and World TB Day details
changelogData.beta_0_083:
Fixed Global Opacity Architecture (Final): Removed global Opacity wrapper; text/icons no longer fade
Added AppState surfaceOpacity/cardColor/bgColor helpers; auto-apply opacity when wallpaper active, 1.0 otherwise
Fixed black screen without wallpaper: app_theme scaffoldBackgroundColor uses Color(t.bg); main.dart returns uiContent directly
settings_screen uses Color(tc.bg) background (Opaque Route) to prevent wallpaper bleed and black transitions
Bulk update across screens: Color(tc.card/bg) → state.cardColor/bgColor; opacity only applies to surfaces, text remains opaque
changelogData.beta_0_082:
Refactored Opacity Architecture (3rd Attempt): When wallpaper active, Stack=[Layer0 Wallpaper 100%]+[Layer1 Opacity(UI)]+[Layer2 Weather Particles]
When no wallpaper, returns Scaffold directly (no Opacity) for zero overhead
Weather particles moved out of Opacity (Layer2) so they are unaffected; reverted opacity patches in today/stats/pomodoro/quadrant
Updated settings text: Lower values = more transparent; cards/AppBar/text fade together to show wallpaper; 100% is opaque
changelogData.beta_0_081:
Fixed Global Opacity Architecture: 3-layer Stack — Layer0 Wallpaper (100%), Layer1 Theme Scrim, Layer2 Transparent Scaffold with opaque content
Opacity semantic: Scrim opacity 1.0 = covers wallpaper; 0.5 = 50% wallpaper visible; text/cards/content always 100% opaque
Removed AppTheme.themeData(opacity) approach (Scaffold.backgroundColor in sub-scaffolds was blocking transparency)
Stats Bento: Swapped Deep Focus (1x2) and Flow Index (1x1) positions
changelogData.beta_0_080:
Changelog Fix: Filled missing beta_0_052 to beta_0_078 entries and corrected beta_0_079 order; now continuous 0.052 to 0.080
MAINTENANCE.md updated with changelog standards (8.1-8.7)
Clarified prohibition on renaming old versions, back-filling, or skipping versions
Clarified false positive on brace counting: '}' inside strings; Dart compiler handles AST; braces are balanced
changelogData.beta_0_079:
Fixed Global Opacity (previous method only changed Scaffold background; internal cards/nav remained opaque/dark)
New approach: AppTheme.themeData(opacity) multiplies all surface colors by opacity; text remains alpha=FF
main.dart passes globalOpacity to AppTheme.themeData(); Scaffold.backgroundColor=transparent when wallpaper active
Result: At 50% opacity, cards/AppBar/Nav are translucent while text remains sharp; wallpaper brightness unaffected
changelogData.beta_0_078: Build fix: _FlowDetailPage description contained actual newline; replaced with \\n escape
changelogData.beta_0_077:
Stats Bento Layout: Habit Tracking 2x1 -> 1x1; Deep Focus 2x1 -> 1x2; Added Flow Index 1x1
_HabitBrick rewritten as compact 1x1
_DeepFocusBrick rewritten as tall 1x2
Added _FlowBrick (1x1) with stage emoji/avg/wave; async load FocusQualityService
Added _FlowDetailPage: Algorithm explanation + 30d trend + 4 recommendations
changelogData.beta_0_085:
Refactored i18n core architecture with Headless UI isolation layer
Implemented multi-level fallback mechanism to prevent raw key leakage
Completed 70+ missing translation entries for better coverage
Established automated i18n verification for long-term stability
